evidence: The paper evaluates variable importance using "mean square error in out-of-bag data (permutation importance) for random forest" rather than impurity-based importance. Although the conclusions rest on importance rankings (e.g., AET identified as "the most important factor," regional variables described as "outperforming" others), the impurity-importance bias does not apply because permutation importance was used. Corroboration is provided through partial residual plots and cross-method comparison (OLS, GAM, random forest).
